tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, indicating possible nephropathy. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity and marked ketonuria, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Metabolic acidosis is suggested by low CO₂. Findings are consistent with acute or chronic kidney disease, possibly with dehydration or catabolic state. Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
